optionList.content: The person is facilitating the accomplishment of group goals.
The person is not taking the group seriously.
The person most likely is consistently late to the group meetings without notice.
The person most likely feels unlucky to be in the group situation.
The person is facilitating the accomplishment of group goals.
The person is not taking the group seriously.
The person most likely is consistently late to the group meetings without notice.
The person most likely feels unlucky to be in the group situation.

studentQuestionArray.content: A work group meeting of registered nurses is in progress and tensions are running high. One of the group members makes a humorous comment at an opportune moment and successfully neutralizes the environment. What conclusion can be reached about the behavior of this group member?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The person plays the functional group-building role of tension reliever. This person uses humor and mediation when group tensions rise and interfere with the group process and accomplishment of tasks.
Option 2:The person’s behavior does not demonstrate that he or she is not taking the group seriously. Rather, the nonfunctional group role of playboy does not take the group seriously. While the playboy is the joker of the group, he or she does not use humor to diffuse group tensions but causes major disruption in the group process.
Option 3:The person assuming the nonfunctional group role of playboy does not take the group seriously and is likely to be consistently late to group meetings without notice. The playboy is the joker of the group, but he or she does not use humor to diffuse group tensions. Instead, this person causes major disruption in the group process.
Option 4:The person assuming the nonfunctional group role of zipper mouth is the silent individual who sits quietly, feeling unlucky to be in the group or even present in the situation. This person does not track the meeting content, and is not likely to use humor to diffuse tensions.

optionList.content: Ensuring that all members have contributed to the discussion
Identifying barriers or blocks to group progress that are occurring
Using humor and mediation when group tensions rise
Reinforcing the standards or processes for effective group functioning
Ensuring that all members have contributed to the discussion
Identifying barriers or blocks to group progress that are occurring
Using humor and mediation when group tensions rise
Reinforcing the standards or processes for effective group functioning

studentQuestionArray.content: Which action is taken by the gatekeeper in a group?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The gatekeeper ensures that all members have contributed to the discussion and that the group is not being monopolized by the views of more verbal members.
Option 2:The diagnoser identifies barriers or blocks to group progress that occur in the group.
Option 3:The tension reliever uses humor and mediation when group tensions rise and interfere with the group process and accomplishment of tasks.
Option 4:The standard setter reinforces the standards or processes for effective group functioning.

optionList.content: Both verbal and nonverbal listener responses
Both verbal and nonverbal listener responses

studentQuestionArray.content: What form of communication can be categorized as a feedback?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Remaining silent does not attribute to feedback as feedback has to be verbal and/or non-verbal.
Option 2:Feedback also includes nonverbal communication. The receiver, on receiving the message and interpreting the same, may respond by providing feedback.
Option 3:Feedback also includes verbal communication. The receiver may be stimulated to respond either by words or action.
Option 4:Feedback may be verbal, nonverbal, or both. Once the receiver has received and interpreted the message, he or she may be stimulated to respond by providing feedback to the sender.

optionList.content: “Maybe I will get to it, when I have more time.”
“Maybe I will get to it, when I have more time.”

studentQuestionArray.content: Which statement made by a nurse manager shows effective leadership?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:This statement shows that the person has courage and optimism. An effective leader is likely to look at an issue as a challenge that needs to be overcome, and not as a problem.
Option 2:An effective leader would be optimistic and will say “yes” to a challenge or task, and not procrastinate or leave it for the future when he or she might have more time.
Option 3:An effective ruler will have a positive and optimistic attitude. He or she would not display a negative attitude and declare that a task is impossible to perform.
Option 4:An effective leader would be enthusiastic and energetic and would declare something as “great” rather than as simply “nice.”

Test Taking Tips: Understand that optimism is a desirable quality in a leader.

optionList.content: “The zero-based budget uses experiences to predict the future.”
“There is a baseline sum that is automatically approved in this budget type.”
“This is a popular method of budgeting in the current times.”
“This type of budget eventually turns into an incremental budget with justifications.”
“The zero-based budget uses experiences to predict the future.”
“There is a baseline sum that is automatically approved in this budget type.”
“This is a popular method of budgeting in the current times.”
“This type of budget eventually turns into an incremental budget with justifications.”

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse instructor is teaching a student nurse about zero-based budgets. Which statement by the student nurse indicates effective learning?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:An incremental or historical budget uses experience to predict the future. It takes the budget from the previous year and adds or deletes money from the various items listed.
Option 2:There is no baseline sum that is automatically approved in zero-based budgeting. Each item in the budget has to be justified and approved each time the budget process is repeated.
Option 3:Zero-based budgeting was popular for a time but is rarely used now.
Option 4:This statement by the student nurse indicates effective learning. In zero-based budgeting, each item in the budget has to be justified and approved each time the budget process is repeated. The items in most unit budgets remain consistent over time. So, after the first year of time-consuming development of rationales for each item, they can be reused in the next year’s budget. The zero-based budget then turns into an incremental budget with justifications.

Test Taking Tips: Recall that most nurse managers work with a budget where money is added to, or deleted from, the items listed in the previous year’s budget to arrive at the new budget.

optionList.content: Winning the approval of everyone around you
Eliminating all of your listeners" physiological noise
Winning the approval of everyone around you
Eliminating all of your listeners" physiological noise

studentQuestionArray.content: What is the benefit of learning to communicate with others?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Effectiveness of communication depends on various factors such as gender, environment, personal space, and so on. Simply learning to communicate does not prevent misunderstanding.
Option 2:Learning to communicate enables a person to establish rewarding relationships.
Option 3:Just by communicating to others does not get the approval of everyone.
Option 4:Physiological noise refers to a person who is preoccupied with his or her own thoughts and such thoughts may affect his or her ability to understand. Learning to communicate has no effect on such noises.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which model of client care divides the nurses into charge nurse, medical nurse, and treatment nurse?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:A model in which each person performs a specific job that is narrowly defined according to the needs of the unit characterizes functional nursing. The nurse manager in this organizational unit is called the charge nurse, and other nurses are grouped into categories such as medical nurse and treatment nurse based on the task they perform.
Option 2:In team nursing, the nurses are called team leader and team member.
Option 3:In primary care nursing, the nurses are called primary nurse and associate nurse.
Option 4:In modular nursing, the nurses form care pairs.

optionList.content: “It’s not all shifts that are short-staffed. Mine is the one that’s always short-staffed with temps, but I am able to orient them successfully after all.”
“It is not fair! I always have to work overtime!”
“I believe all of us feel stressed and burned out by our current shift timings.”
“This is ridiculous! You are wrong about this and you don’t have the sense to know it!”
“It’s not all shifts that are short-staffed. Mine is the one that’s always short-staffed with temps, but I am able to orient them successfully after all.”
“It is not fair! I always have to work overtime!”
“I believe all of us feel stressed and burned out by our current shift timings.”
“This is ridiculous! You are wrong about this and you don’t have the sense to know it!”

studentQuestionArray.content: In a work-group meeting of registered nurses, one nurse plays the role of the expresser. Which statement is this nurse most likely to make?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:This statement is most likely to have been made by a recognition seeker. The recognition seeker is a nonfunctional group role where the individual consistently attempts to draw the group’s attention to his or her personal beliefs, values, and concerns.
Option 2:This statement is likely to be made by a monopolizer. This person seeks attention and demands that the group focus on him or her. He or she may repeatedly interrupt others and perceive his or her issues and problems as the most important.
Option 3:This statement is likely to be made by an expresser. The expresser is a functional group-building role, where the individual restates or identifies and expresses the feelings of the group.
Option 4:This statement is most likely to be made by the aggressor. This is a nonfunctional group role, where the individual attacks during the discussion, criticizing other group members because they do not have the same insights or experiences from his or her perspective.

optionList.content: Ability to tolerate the consequences of taking risks
Ability to tolerate the consequences of taking risks

studentQuestionArray.content: Which trait distinguishing a leader from a nonleader is most often identified in the trait theories of leadership?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Intelligence is one of the traits identified most often in the trait theories of leadership.
Option 2:Creativity is one of the traits identified in the trait theories of leadership, but it is not one of the most identified traits.
Option 3:Ability to tolerate the consequences of taking is one of the traits identified in the trait theories of leadership, but it is not one of the most identified traits.
Option 4:Willingness to take risks is one of the traits identified in the trait theories of leadership, but it is not one of the most identified traits.

studentQuestionArray.content: In a work-group meeting of registered nurses, a nurse says to another group member, “Well done! This is really great work!” Which functional group-building role does the nurse play?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse plays the role of an encourager. This is a functional group-building role where the person praises contributions of the group and other group members.
Option 2:The nurse does not play the role of the energizer. This is a functional task role where the person stimulates the group into action toward the goals either by introducing certain issues or topics or by his/her behavior.
Option 3:The nurse does not play the role of the summarizer. This is a functional task role where the person summarizes suggestions, actions, and accomplishments that have occurred in the group.
Option 4:The nurse does not play the role of the expresser. This is a functional group-building role where the person restates or identifies and expresses the feelings of the group.

optionList.content: Every member of the group participates in all decision-making.
The members of the group are given feedback only when they are doing something incorrectly.
The members of the group can sometimes face harsh criticism from the leader.
Every member of the group is closely monitored and micro-managed.
Every member of the group participates in all decision-making.
The members of the group are given feedback only when they are doing something incorrectly.
The members of the group can sometimes face harsh criticism from the leader.
Every member of the group is closely monitored and micro-managed.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which is a characteristic of democratic leadership?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:In the democratic style of leadership, every member of the group needs to participate in all decision-making.
Option 2:In the laissez-faire style of leadership, the members of the group are given feedback when they are doing something incorrectly; they do not receive any direction on how to do the task correctly.
Option 3:In the dictatorial style of leadership, which is an extreme form of authoritarian leadership, the leader is only concerned about achieving the goal and will use any means, including harsh criticism, to do so.
Option 4:Leaders who exhibit authoritarian leadership are micro-managers closely monitor everything that the group members do and often make on-the-spot changes when they believe they know a better way to achieve a task or goal.

Test Taking Tips: Recall that the authoritarian leader provides direction by giving orders that the group be expected to carry out without question.

optionList.content: The staff nurses are guaranteed to increase productivity.
The staff nurses may not increase productivity.
The staff nurses will be led to focus more on the environment than the work at hand.
The staff nurses will be motivated to produce higher quality work.
The staff nurses are guaranteed to increase productivity.
The staff nurses may not increase productivity.
The staff nurses will be led to focus more on the environment than the work at hand.
The staff nurses will be motivated to produce higher quality work.

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse manager is tasked with increasing the overall productivity and performance quality of the nursing staff. The nurse manager knows that many of the staff nurses feel underpaid, and plans to increase their salary. What result can be expected from this action?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Salary is a hygiene factor, or a needs dissatisfier. It is a negative motivator, and when it is not met, work productivity is reduced. However, if it is satisfied, there is no guarantee that increased productivity and higher-quality performance will result.
Option 2:Salary is a hygiene factor, or a needs dissatisfier. It is a negative motivator, and when it is not met, work productivity is reduced. When this need is met, it may not increase productivity and lead to higher-quality performance.
Option 3:Salary is a hygiene factor. When these needs are not met, the person feels dissatisfied with his or her job and focuses more on the environment than the work that is supposed to be performed. This need being met will not result in the staff nurses focusing more on the environment than the work at hand.
Option 4:When needs motivators are satisfied, they can encourage workers to increase their productivity and deliver higher-quality work. Salary, however, is a hygiene factor, or a needs dissatisfier. When not met, this need will decrease productivity, and when it is met, there is no guarantee that the person will be motivated to produce higher quality work.

Test Taking Tips: Understand that in terms of a needs dissatisfier, productivity does not necessarily increase when the need is satisfied.

studentQuestionArray.content: A registered nurse who leads a work group has been able to motivate the group members so successfully that these nurses have gone beyond their own self-interests for the good of the organization and accomplished more than what was expected of them. Which type of leadership is the nurse most likely to have exhibited?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The group leader exhibited transformational leadership. Transformational leaders communicate their vision in a way that is so meaningful and exciting that the followers are inspired and motivated to go beyond their own self-interests for the good of the group or organization, often accomplishing more than what would normally be expected of them.
Option 2:The group leader is not likely to have exhibited a transactional leadership. Transactional theory assumes that people are motivated by reward and punishment and that they work best within a clear chain of command. People who follow this type of a leader have no reason to be so motivated that they would go beyond their self-interests for the good of the organization.
Option 3:The group leader is not likely to have exhibited situational leadership. The situational theories recognize the complexity of work situations and encourage the leader to consider a number of factors when deciding what action to take. This is not specifically associated with inspiring and motivating the followers.
Option 4:The group leader is not likely to have exhibited task-focused leadership. Task-focused leaders emphasize the tasks and fail to realize that interpersonal relationships have considerable impact on employee morale and productivity. Such a leadership style is not likely to be particularly inspiring or motivating for the followers.

Test Taking Tips: Rewards like salary and benefits are not usually sufficient to motivate people to go beyond their self-interest for the good of the organization.

optionList.content: Restating the feelings of the group
Restating the feelings of the group

studentQuestionArray.content: Which functional group-building role does the expresser play?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The disagreer is a functional task role. The disagreer identifies errors in statements made or proposes a different viewpoint.
Option 2:The diagnoser is a functional group-building role. This person identifies barriers or blocks to group progress that are occurring during the group meetings.
Option 3:The information giver is a functional task role. This person offers pertinent information from personal knowledge appropriate to the group topic or task.
Option 4:The expresser is a functional group-building role. The expresser restates or identifies and expresses the feelings of the group.

studentQuestionArray.content: A group of registered nurses are about to wind down a work group meeting. One of the nurses says, “So, in conclusion, we can say that five of us achieved our targets in this week, and the remaining two will try out the suggestions that have been made in this meeting.” Which functional task role is the nurse playing in this situation?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse is not playing the role of the information giver. The information giver offers pertinent information from personal knowledge appropriate to the group topic or task.
Option 2:The nurse is not playing the role of the consensus taker. The consensus taker seeks the weight of group sentiments or consensus on the issues. This is a functional group-building role, and not a functional task role, as differentiated by Tappen.
Option 3:The nurse is playing the functional role of the summarizer. The summarizer summarizes suggestions, actions, and accomplishments that have occurred in the group.
Option 4:The nurse is not playing the role of the elaborator. The elaborator expands on suggestions already made.

Test Taking Tips: Understand that the nurse making this statement is not adding any new point to the discussion.

studentQuestionArray.content: What does the phrase “decode a message” refer to?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Reject refers to non-acceptance of the message. The message is discarded and not decoded.
Option 2:Evaluate refers to judging a message or forming an idea from the message.
Option 3:Interpretation is also called decoding. It refers to relating the message to receiver’s past experiences to determine the sender’s meaning.
Option 4:Encode refers to translation of idea into code.

optionList.content: Distorts or interferes with a message
Causes listeners to listen to messages more carefully
Distorts or interferes with a message
Causes listeners to listen to messages more carefully

studentQuestionArray.content: What is the effect of noise in the communication process?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Noise causes confusion and does not enhance a message.
Option 2:Noise causes distraction and seldom helps in focusing.
Option 3:Background noise is distracting, interferes with hearing, and can create confusion.
Option 4:Noise interferes with hearing, hence it causes confusion.

Test Taking Tips: Before choosing an option think if a noisy environment is a good environment for communication or not.

optionList.content: Ignore their behavior completely and walk away
Use direct and aggressive communication when they are being difficult
Focus on issues without attacking their personality
Ignore their behavior completely and walk away
Use direct and aggressive communication when they are being difficult
Focus on issues without attacking their personality

studentQuestionArray.content: Which is an effective technique for communicating with a persecutor?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Persecutors want people to leave them alone so that they can continue their behavior without confrontation. So while walking away when the persecutor is in a highly animated state is recommended, it is good to walk back when he or she has calmed down and identify the behavior to him or her calmly and directly.
Option 2:Communication with a persecutor needs to be assertive but never aggressive. Yelling back or arguing with a persecutor is not helpful.
Option 3:Attempting to destroy the persecutor’s self-image is not helpful. Persecutors have fragile egos and attacking them will only invite a more vicious counterattack.
Option 4:It is beneficial to separate the person from the difficult behavior by focusing on the issues without attacking their personality. Having the facts about the specific behavior to be addressed makes people much more receptive to resolution of the problem than attacking their personality.

studentQuestionArray.content: During a meeting, a registered nurse who is a part of a work group asks, “Do we all agree on this point made by our colleague?” Which functional group-building role is played by this nurse?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse does not play the role of information seeker. It is not a functional group-building role, but a functional task role, as identified by Tappen.
Option 2:The nurse plays the role of the consensus taker. In this role, the nurse seeks the weight of group sentiments or consensus on the issues discussed in the meeting.
Option 3:The nurse does not play the role of the expresser. In the role of expresser, the nurse would restate or identify and express the feelings of the group.
Option 4:The nurse does not play the role of the follower. In the follower role, the nurse would consent to whatever is proposed by others in the group and not demonstrate active participation in the discussion.

optionList.content: Feeling frustrated due to lack of direction
Harboring hostile feelings that they are afraid to express
Feeling an inability to use their full potential as workers
Feeling frustrated due to lack of direction
Harboring hostile feelings that they are afraid to express
Feeling an inability to use their full potential as workers

studentQuestionArray.content: Which types of feelings and behaviors are usually seen in people who work under authoritarian leaders? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:People who work under laissez-faire leaders often feel frustrated due to lack of direction from the leaders.
Option 2:People who work under authoritarian leaders usually harbor hostile feelings that they are fearful to express.
Option 3:People who work under authoritarian leaders use passive-aggressive techniques to try to even the playing field.
Option 4:People who work under authoritarian leaders feel oppressed and unable to use their full potential as a worker.
Option 5:People who work under benevolent leaders often feel that they are being condescended or patronized. While this is a type of authoritarian leadership, these feelings in group members are seen only in benevolent leadership, and not in general authoritarian leadership.

Test Taking Tips: The laissez-faire leader prefers not to get too involved with the workings of the group.
